purpose: Prove the opt-in Triton SwiGLU owner-rank VJP backend is wired for the normalized 4xMI350 S4096 target slice before running an uncontaminated training smoke.
check_config_command_shape: IMAGE=onenexus/nexus-titan:rocm722-pytorch-nightly-mori REMOTE_ROOT=/scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527 GPUS_PER_NODE=4 CANARY_EP=4 CANARY_GBS=64 CANARY_SEQ_LEN=4096 CANARY_STEPS=2 CANARY_CHECKPOINT_ENABLE=false CANARY_ACTIVATION_CHECKPOINT_MODE=full CANARY_MOE_COMM_BACKEND=mori CANARY_EXPERIMENTAL_MORI_AITER_MOE=1 CANARY_MORI_AITER_BACKWARD_MODE=routed CANARY_MORI_AITER_LOCAL_VJP_MODE=padded CANARY_MORI_AITER_LOCAL_VJP_SWIGLU_BACKEND=triton CANARY_MORI_SHMEM_HEAP_SIZE=32G launchers/torchtitan_direct_deepseek_v4_4x8xmi350_canary.sh check-config

node_state_before_training.reason: The node had active external workloads on both 4-GPU halves: a Qwen3 fault-tolerance TorchTitan job using ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES=4,5,6,7 and a Primus/TorchTitan Qwen3-30B-A3B elastic run with large resident GPU memory on the other half, plus active AITER compilation. Starting a target-shape DSv4 run would contaminate correctness and performance evidence.
node_state_before_training.observed_gpu_state_summary: cards 0-3 had about 54-55% VRAM allocated; card4 was active; rocm-smi reported active Python/pt_elastic PIDs tied to the external containers.

direct_triton_xgrad_reduce.read: Correct but much slower than padded torch; do not integrate direct FP32 atomic xgrad+reduce.

simple_triton_w2_dgrad_swiglu.read: Correct but slower than padded torch; keep as an oracle for a CK/TileLang grouped training-GEMM schedule.

next_action: When do-sonle5-mi350-gpu is idle, run the normalized 4x S4096 GBS64 full-AC training smoke with CANARY_MORI_AITER_LOCAL_VJP_SWIGLU_BACKEND=triton. For further MoE closure, avoid the already-negative direct Triton xgrad/W2 schedules and target CK/TileLang or another MFMA-efficient grouped training-GEMM backend for owner-rank wgrad/xgrad.
